name: Andrew Basiago Claims
description: Andrew Basiago is an American lawyer who has gained notoriety for his claims of participating in secret U.S. government time travel and teleportation programs, primarily Project Pegasus, during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He alleges that these programs involved traveling through time and space, including trips to Mars. Basiago asserts that he encountered historical figures like Abraham Lincoln and interacted with future versions of himself. He further claims to have been part of a U.S. effort to identify and train children with psychic abilities for future government service. He suggests these programs were intended for intelligence gathering, technological development, and perhaps even establishing a presence on Mars. The mainstream scientific community overwhelmingly rejects Basiago's claims, citing a lack of verifiable evidence, inconsistencies in his accounts, and the absence of corroborating testimony from credible sources. Physicists point to the theoretical impossibilities of time travel as described by Basiago. Historians question the plausibility of his alleged interactions with historical figures. Skeptics often highlight the lack of concrete proof and the reliance on anecdotal evidence in Basiago's narratives. Furthermore, government agencies have not corroborated his claims, and no official documentation has surfaced to support his assertions. His claims are generally categorized within the realm of conspiracy theories and pseudoscience.
subtitle: Alleged Time Travel and Mars Exploration

claims.evidence: Basiago's personal testimony, alleged photographs of himself on Mars, and purported recollections of his experiences.
claims.criticism: Lack of corroborating evidence, inconsistencies in Basiago's accounts, absence of official documentation, and the implausibility of time travel as described.

claims.claim: Travel to Mars as a child
claims.evidence: Basiago's description of the Mars environment, alleged encounters with extraterrestrial beings, and purported photographs of himself on Mars.
claims.criticism: Dubious authenticity of the photographs, lack of independent verification of his Mars experiences, and the unlikelihood of the U.S. government sending children on such missions.

claims.claim: Encounters with historical figures like Abraham Lincoln
claims.evidence: Basiago's accounts of these meetings and his recollections of conversations with Lincoln.
claims.criticism: Lack of historical corroboration, the improbability of such encounters, and the absence of any other eyewitness accounts.

keyFigures.name: Andrew Basiago
keyFigures.role: Primary claimant and proponent of the time travel and Mars exploration narrative.

keyFigures.name: Alfred Webre
keyFigures.role: Advocate and supporter of Basiago's claims, promoting them through Exopolitics.

keyFigures.name: Michael Salla
keyFigures.role: Another advocate and supporter of Basiago's claims, promoting them through Exopolitics.

mythologyConnections: Possible parallels to myths of journeys to other worlds and encounters with supernatural beings.
Themes of secret knowledge and hidden technologies.

timeline.event: Basiago begins publicly sharing his claims.
Continued promotion of Basiago's claims through various media outlets.

sources.author: Alfred Webre
sources.work: Exopolitics: Politics, Government, and Law in the Universe
sources.title: Alfred Webre
